title: IaP Execution Plan v1
description: Schema for the deterministic plan artifact (apiVersion plan.iap.dev/v1) per IEP-0011 and Chapter 14. planId is the SHA-256 of the canonical serialization of `content` (UTF-8 JSON, keys sorted by Unicode code point, waves ordered by dependency rank, entries within a wave sorted by resource identifier — CP-2); the envelope carries timestamps and the signature and is never hashed into planId (Chapter 14 §14.5: no timestamps inside a plan). Plan bytes are a closed contract: unlike document-side schemas there is no x-* passthrough anywhere — any additional content would silently change planId and break approval binding.

apiVersion.description: Plan artifact format identifier; always plan.iap.dev/v1.

planId.description: SHA-256 over the canonical serialization of the `content` member (and nothing else), spelled sha256:<hex>. Approvals bind to this identifier (Chapter 14 §14.5).

envelope.description: Signature, expiry, and audit metadata AROUND the hashed plan content — excluded from planId by construction; integrity comes from the signature (IEP-0011). Optional: an unsigned planner core emits no envelope; signing and expiry derivation are the enveloping planner's concern.

createdAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC time the plan was produced. Lives only here — plan content contains no timestamps (PL-5).

expiresAt.description: RFC 3339 UTC time after which an engine MUST refuse the plan; derived from the earliest validity horizon of the discovery/pricing snapshots, capped by configuration (IEP-0011).

signature.description: Detached ed25519 signature over the canonical serialization of { createdAt, expiresAt, planId } (compact UTF-8 JSON, keys sorted by Unicode code point). Because planId is the SHA-256 of the canonical content serialization, the signature binds the envelope timestamps to the exact content bytes, making both content and expiry tamper-evident; engines verify it against a trusted planner key before execution (PL-2).

keyId.description: Identifier resolved against the verifier's trust store; unknown key ids refuse the plan.

alg.description: Signature algorithm; only ed25519 is defined for v1.

value.description: Base64-encoded ed25519 signature over the canonical { createdAt, expiresAt, planId } signing form.

content.description: The hashed plan content: everything an engine executes and an approval binds to. Canonical serialization of exactly this member yields planId.

inputs.description: The closed nine-element determinism input vector of IEP-0011, each element identified/hashed, plus inputsHash over their canonical serialization. Anything else influencing a plan is a conformance failure (CP-1, PL-5).

documentHash.description: Identity 1a: SHA-256 of the canonical IaP document (C1–C6, active profile merged), spelled sha256:<hex>.

target.description: Identity 1b: the explicit deployment target the plan was computed for.

provider.description: Provider namespace the plan targets (the mapping artifact's provider field).

profile.description: Active profile the canonical form is relative to; null for the base document.

profileHashes.description: Identity 2: profile name to SHA-256 hash (sha256:<hex>) of each merged profile definition.

policyBundles.description: Identity 3: policy bundle name to version or hash identifying the bundle in force.

extensionVersions.description: Identity 4: extension package namespace to version.

mappingVersions.description: Identity 5: provider mapping namespace to mapping artifact version (semver).

discoverySnapshot.description: Identity 6: provider discovery snapshot id (pre-fetched, versioned observations — Chapter 12 §12.2 forbids ambient lookups); null when no snapshot participates.

pricingSnapshot.description: Identity 7: pricing snapshot id cost deltas are computed from (Chapter 20 §20.2.2); null when none is supplied, in which case deltas.cost is explicitly unavailable.

stateRevision.description: Identity 8a: monotonic revision of the infrastructure-model state document the plan was computed against (IEP-0010); 0 for the empty snapshot.

stateIntegrity.description: Identity 8b: integrity hash of the state snapshot's objects, spelled sha256:<hex>.

plannerVersion.description: Identity 9: semver of the planning implementation (includes the risk rule-table version per phase-7 design decision 8).

inputsHash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical serialization of the nine identities above (all inputs properties except inputsHash itself), spelled sha256:<hex>. Engines recompute all identities and refuse the plan on any mismatch (PL-2).

waves.description: The execution schedule: wave n contains every scheduled node whose longest dependency path within the execution graph has length n (Chapter 14 §14.4). Forward waves (create/update-in-place/replace/import, Kahn layering over dependsOn) come first; delete waves follow in reverse dependency order (§14.3). Within a wave, entries are sorted lexicographically by resource identifier (CP-2).

items.description: One wave: scheduled actions with no ordering edges between them, executable concurrently.

destructiveActions.description: Every replace and delete in the plan, listed with its reversibility class — always present, even when empty; absence of a destructive action from this list is a conformance failure (PL-3). Sorted by resource identifier.

resource.description: Logical id of the affected provider resource.

reversibility.description: Reversibility class of the action (Chapter 14 §14.6; roadmap rollback classes).

unknownValues.description: Attributes resolvable only at apply time: output-bound attributes generated by the substrate, and attributes declared sensitive by the provider plan — marked here instead of leaking values (CE-6, PL-5). Sorted by (resource, attribute, reason).

items.description: One late-bound or redacted attribute marker.

resource.description: Logical id of the provider resource carrying the attribute.

attribute.description: Provider attribute path on that resource.

reason.description: Why the value is unknown in the plan: output-binding (generated at apply time, bound to an abstract output attribute) or sensitive (value deliberately never enters plan content).

risk.description: Deterministic risk annotation: a pure function of the plan content (everything except this member), computed by the versioned rule table identified through plannerVersion (IEP-0011).

score.description: Ordinal risk score: an integer sum of factor weights; never floating point.

factors.description: The individual weighted factors the score sums, sorted by id.

id.description: Stable rule identifier from the versioned risk rule table.

weight.description: Integer weight this factor contributes to the score.

resources.description: Logical ids of the resources the factor applies to, sorted.

deltas.description: Honest cost/security/compliance deltas (phase-7 design decision 5): explicit unavailable/deferred markers instead of fabricated numbers.

cost.description: Cost delta status. unavailable when no pricing snapshot id was supplied (never a silent zero — IEP-0011 cost impact); deferred when a snapshot is pinned but pricing computation has not landed (Phase 10).

status.description: Whether a cost delta could be computed.

reason.description: Machine-readable reason token for the status.

pricingSnapshot.description: The pinned pricing snapshot id, present when status is deferred.

security.description: Security-relevant changes derived from the plan itself: scheduled fields whose canonical source path lies under spec.exposure or spec.encryption. Sorted by (resource, field).

resource.description: Logical id of the affected provider resource.

source.description: Canonical document field path (spec.exposure.* or spec.encryption.*) the attribute derives from.

compliance.description: Compliance delta: empty with an explicit deferred marker until the Phase 11 compliance engine lands (design decision 5).

deferred.description: Marker naming the phase that supplies compliance deltas.

findings.description: Compliance delta findings; always empty while deferred.

items.description: One compliance delta finding (shape reserved for Phase 11).

resource.description: Logical id of the affected provider resource.

rollback.description: Rollback contract: rollback is a plan like any other (Chapter 14 §14.6) — the strategy is re-planning to a prior succeeded revision; limitations name actions that cannot be rolled back without data loss or manual recovery.

strategy.description: The only defined rollback strategy: select a prior succeeded revision and re-plan to its documentHash (§14.6, no second code path).

limitations.description: Explicit per-resource rollback limitations (e.g. delete of a stateful resource without a restore source). Sorted by resource.

resource.description: Logical id of the resource the limitation applies to.

reason.description: Why rollback is limited for this resource.

verification.description: Post-apply verification steps: one entry per scheduled resource, checking the action's outcome. Sorted by resource.

resource.description: Logical id of the resource to verify.

check.description: The verification to perform, keyed by action class: created (create), attributes-applied (update-in-place), successor-ready (replace), adopted (import), removed (delete).

approvalsRequired.description: Approval gates that must be satisfied before execution (Chapter 19 §19.6); populated by risk scoring. Always present; sorted by (resource, gate).

resource.description: Logical id of the resource the gate protects.

sha256Digest.description: SHA-256 digest spelled sha256:<64 lowercase hex digits>.

semver.description: Semantic version: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional prerelease.

rfc3339.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (UTC or numeric offset). Permitted only inside the envelope, never in content (PL-5).

logicalId.description: Deterministic provider-plan resource identity: <resourceId>.<targetType> (IEP-0012 provider plan shape).

fieldPath.description: Attribute or canonical document field path (dot-separated).

reversibilityClass.description: Closed reversibility vocabulary (Chapter 14 §14.6; roadmap Phase 14 rollback classes).

identityElement.description: Name of a determinism-input identity element (the keys of content.inputs, excluding inputsHash).

actionEntry.description: One scheduled lifecycle action for one provider resource.

resource.description: Logical id of the provider resource the action applies to.

action.description: Lifecycle action class per Chapter 14 §14.2 plus import (IEP-0010 adoption; an unmanaged existing object is never blindly updated).

fields.description: Attribute paths the action touches, sorted: every desired attribute for create; changed/added/removed attributes for update-in-place and replace (plus the pseudo-field `type` on a provider-type change); drifted attributes for import; empty for delete (the action is total). Names only — never values (PL-5).

provenance.description: Why the action exists: the determinism-input identity that caused it and, per touched field, the mapping-level source recorded in the provider plan's per-attribute provenance.

changedBy.description: The input identity responsible for the change: documentHash for create/update-in-place/replace/delete (intent changed), stateRevision for import (state contains an unmanaged object).

fieldSources.description: Touched field path to its source: `constant` (mapping constant), `from:<canonical path>` (verbatim carry-over), `map:<canonical path>` (mapped lookup), or `state` (the path exists only in the state snapshot).

destructive.description: True exactly for replace and delete actions; every destructive entry also appears in destructiveActions (PL-3).
